note: Deterministic mutation operators. The three v1 operators are TOP-LEVEL only and are still applied by validate_negative_fixtures.py; R4 finding B7 was that top-level-only mutation made '208 mutants, 0 survivors' sound far stronger than it was. The eighteen v2 families are applied by scripts/validate_recursive_mutations.py, which walks every positive example part RECURSIVELY, resolving the effective schema at each node (through $ref, allOf and the applicable if/then and anyOf/oneOf branch) so it knows which fields are required, which arrays are sets, and which values are references. No randomness anywhere: mutants are enumerated in sorted path order, so the corpus and the report are byte-stable.
kill_criterion: A mutant is KILLED if the hardened JSON Schema rejects the mutated part, or if validate_cross_record_semantics.collect_issues flags the mutated bundle. Every unmutated example bundle is issue-free, so any issue a mutant raises is attributable to the mutation.

operators.description: remove each top-level required field, one at a time
set each top-level enum-constrained field to the value 'NOT-A-LEGAL-VALUE'
add one undeclared top-level field '__mutant__': true
delete each required field at EVERY depth, not only the top level
blank each required string; '' is not a declaration
empty each required array or map the contract requires to be nonempty
duplicate a member of an array-as-set, and collide two ids that must differ
repoint each currently-resolvable reference (claim, token, evidence, episode) at nothing
desynchronise an analysis version between records that must share it
desynchronise an occurrence version between records that must share it
delete the field a residual's own disposition makes mandatory
make a claim depend on itself, directly or through another claim
delete the status of a verdict the required path demands
state an episode- or claim-level pathway summary the statuses do not support
strip the reason from a not-applicable status, anywhere in the record
give one token several of_type entries (the unimplemented many-to-many MetaInst)
empty the binding map: a token with no material binding
declare a RelSpec at or after the result it is used to assess
vary a field the PerturbSpec declares invariant
collapse a candidate SET of complete profiles into one partial profile (finding B1)
overwrite a claim's reasoning adequacy with the episode-level pathway state (finding B2)

expectation: Every generated mutant is killed at a declared layer, OR is listed below as a justified equivalent with a reason why the mutation does not change what the record claims. An undeclared survivor fails the run.
